required_changes: [gem route balance] Rebuild direct resource region [7,1]->[8,1]: split the resources into staged sub-regions and add pressure on the easiest internal route before the richest rewards.

summary: current: MT1 is a valid 13x13 floor with allowed tiles/enemies, no orthogonal enemy adjacency, exact MT1 resource progression, and tool usage within remaining whole-tower budget. The inferred left, central, and right/tool routes provide distinct door, combat, and reward tradeoffs with protected high-value rewards. | connectivity density: Connectivity/density review passes: wall_ratio 0.515 and monster_density_non_wall 0.207 support compact pressure, main_cycle_rank_ratio 0.203 is not an uncontrolled open grid, and the minimum exit route has meaningful pressure with 3 enemies plus substantial door/key cost. No special-monster avoidance issue applies. | resource balance: Resource access is acceptable: the entrance exposes no resources or tools, low-cost clusters are small and route-gated, blue doors unlock stronger rewards or strategic access, and the centerFly/pickaxe pocket is protected by meaningful upper-right route cost. | monster damage: Monster pacing is acceptable for MT1: the minimum exit route uses bat (6,11), blackSlime (3,3), and skeleton (5,1), giving a clear early/moderate/exit combat ramp without impossible fights. Optional branches add stronger threshold checks through zombie (3,5), zombie (11,3), skeleton (7,3), and skeleton (9,7), while weaker greenSlime placements are limited and not enough to collapse the floor into filler. No zone or repulse sources are present, so there is no special-damage route issue in this specialist scope. | gem route balance: Gem route balance reviewer found 5 route/resource imbalance issue(s).
